title: ochna puberula
intro: Ochna puberula is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, commonly known as the small-leaf ochna. It is native to tropical Africa and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antidote for snake bites. Herbal preparations of Ochna puberula can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
